mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card behavior on Print and Payment History page when a plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages, additional pages, progress bars, tooltips, and page count displays.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.

components_found_in_this_batch.ui_element_description: Button element that allows users to enroll their printer in the HP Instant Ink subscription service. This button appears on printer boxes for printers that are eligible for Instant Ink but not yet enrolled. Clicking initiates the Instant Ink enrollment flow.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This precondition requires enrolling a printer with a 50+ pages plan. The enroll_in_instant_ink button on the printers management page can be used to initiate the Instant Ink enrollment flow via UI. While this is a UI-based enrollment approach (the test may prefer API-based enrollment), this button provides a way to start the enrollment process if UI enrollment is needed during test setup.

components_found_in_this_batch.ui_element_description: Container box element for a printer that is already enrolled in Instant Ink subscription. Uses a complex selector that matches elements whose class starts with 'styles__PrinterBox-@jarvis/react-hpx-devices' and contains a descendant with data-testid 'printer-supplies'. The presence of the printer-supplies element indicates the printer is enrolled and showing supply/ink level information.
components_found_in_this_batch.usage_example:
printers_page = PrintersPage(page)
assert printers_page.elements.enrolled_printer_box.is_visible(), 'Printer should be enrolled'
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This locator can be used to verify that a printer has been successfully enrolled in Instant Ink after the enrollment process completes. The presence of this element confirms the precondition that the printer is enrolled with a plan. This is useful for validating that the enrollment precondition has been met before proceeding with the test.

components_found_in_this_batch.ui_element_description: Container box element for a printer that is not enrolled in Instant Ink subscription. Uses a complex selector that matches elements whose class starts with 'styles__PrinterBox-@jarvis/react-hpx-devices' and contains a descendant with data-testid 'enroll-II-pp'. The presence of the enroll button indicates the printer is eligible but not yet enrolled in Instant Ink.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This locator can be used to identify printers that are not yet enrolled in Instant Ink. During test setup, this can help verify the initial state before enrollment or identify which printer needs to be enrolled to fulfill the precondition.

precondition_mapping.notes: Found UI components for Instant Ink enrollment on the printers management page. The enroll_in_instant_ink button can initiate enrollment flow, and the enrolled/non-enrolled printer box locators can verify enrollment status. However, no API helper methods for programmatic enrollment were found in this batch. The test case analysis suggests API-based enrollment is preferred for test setup. Also, no components found for selecting the specific plan (50/100/300/700 pages) - the enrollment flow and plan selection page objects are likely in different batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: No components found in this batch for verifying or managing subscription free months information. This would require either API helpers to check subscription details or UI elements on a subscription details/management page. The printers_page.py has a manage_subscription_button but no locators for viewing or verifying free months status. Subscription detail verification components are likely in different batches containing subscription management or API helper modules.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: No components found in this batch for verifying subscription status. This would require either API helpers to retrieve subscription status or UI elements displaying subscription state. The printers_page.py contains printer management UI but no subscription status verification components. Subscription status checking components are likely in different batches containing subscription management pages or API helper modules.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: No components found in this batch for pausing the Instant Ink plan. The printers_page.py has a manage_subscription_button that might navigate to subscription management where pause functionality exists, but no direct 'Pause Plan' button or 'Pause Instant Ink' link locators were found. The CIT modal and pause plan UI components are likely in different batches containing subscription management or dashboard page objects.

matched_page_objects.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and locators for the Printers management page where users can view and manage their registered printers. Provides locators for printer online status indicators, printer options menu, remove printer functionality, Instant Ink enrollment buttons, printer boxes for enrolled and non-enrolled printers, confirmation dialogs for printer removal, and subscription management buttons.
matched_page_objects.relevance_reason: This page object is relevant to Precondition 1 (enrolling printer with Instant Ink plan). The PrintersPage contains the enroll_in_instant_ink button to initiate enrollment and locators to verify enrollment status (enrolled_printer_box and non_enrolled_printer_box). While the test case focuses on the Print and Payment History page, the PrintersPage is needed during test setup to ensure the printer is enrolled with the required plan before navigating to the billing page.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Button element that allows users to enroll their printer in the HP Instant Ink subscription service. This button appears on printer boxes for printers that are eligible for Instant Ink but not yet enrolled. Clicking initiates the Instant Ink enrollment flow.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Relevant to Precondition 1 for enrolling a printer with a 50+ pages Instant Ink plan. This button can be used during test setup to initiate the enrollment flow if UI-based enrollment is needed.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Container box element for a printer that is already enrolled in Instant Ink subscription. The presence of the printer-supplies element indicates the printer is enrolled and showing supply/ink level information.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Relevant to Precondition 1 for verifying that a printer has been successfully enrolled in Instant Ink. Can be used to confirm the enrollment precondition is met before proceeding with the test.

matched_locators.ui_element_description: Container box element for a printer that is not enrolled in Instant Ink subscription. The presence of the enroll button indicates the printer is eligible but not yet enrolled.
matched_locators.relevance_reason: Relevant to Precondition 1 for identifying printers that need enrollment. Can be used during test setup to verify initial state before enrollment.

This batch contains printer management pages (replacement, selection, updates, printers list, privacy banner) but not the Print and Payment History page. Navigation and verification components for the Print and Payment History page are in different batches.

step_level_mapping.step_expected_result: Verify the Complimentary pages progress bar is displayed well.

verification_components_found_in_this_ba.notes: No components found for complimentary pages progress bar.

step_level_mapping.step_action: For Desktop: Move the mouse cursor on the info icon. For Mobile/Tablet: Click on the info icon.
step_level_mapping.step_expected_result: Verify a tooltip with correct message is displayed well.

step_level_mapping.step_expected_result: Verify the value is displayed as "0 of 10(Pause Plan) used".

step_level_mapping.step_expected_result: Verify the information with correct plan info is displayed well.

step_level_mapping.step_action: Print some pages(less than the plan, such as 6 pages). Refresh the dashboard page.
step_level_mapping.step_expected_result: Verify the "Complimentary pages" progress bar is filled with the black color and the value is displayed as correct (such as "6 of 10 used").

verification_components_found_in_this_ba.notes: No components found for page printing simulation or progress bar verification.

step_level_mapping.step_action: Continue to print some pages(more than the plan, such as 15 pages). Refresh the dashboard page.
step_level_mapping.step_expected_result: Verify the progress bars for "Complimentary pages" and "Additional pages" are displayed well.

verification_components_found_in_this_ba.notes: No components found for page printing or progress bar verification.

step_level_mapping.step_expected_result: Verify the progress bar is filled with the Yellow color and the value is displayed correctly(such as "5 of 10 used").

step_level_mapping.step_action: Check the icon. For Desktop: Move the mouse cursor on the info icon. For Mobile/Tablet: Click on the info icon.
step_level_mapping.step_expected_result: Verify the info icon is displayed well behind the string "Additional pages". Verify a tooltip with correct message is displayed well.

verification_components_found_in_this_ba.notes: No components found for additional pages info icon or tooltip verification.

step_level_mapping.step_action: Check the message below "Additional pages". Check the Complimentary pages progress bar. Check total pages printed info.
step_level_mapping.step_expected_result: Verify a message with the amount of blocks already bought is displayed well. Verify the "Complimentary pages" progress bar is full filled with the black color and the value is displayed correctly (such as "10 of 10 used"). Verify correct number(such as 15 pages) displayed according to the printed pages.

verification_components_found_in_this_ba.notes: No components found for additional pages message, progress bar, or total pages verification.

step_level_mapping.step_action: Verify the test case is complete.

verification_components_found_in_this_ba.notes: This is a summary step with no specific components needed.

test_case_mappings.batch_coverage_summary: This batch contains printer management page objects (printer replacement, printer selection, printer updates, printers list, and privacy banner) but does NOT contain the Print and Payment History page which is the main focus of test case C44873414. The test case is about verifying the Billing Cycle Period card behavior on the Print and Payment History page, including complimentary pages progress bars, additional pages progress bars, tooltips, and page count displays. None of these UI elements are present in this batch. The only relevant components found are related to Precondition 1 (printer enrollment): the enroll_in_instant_ink button and enrolled/non-enrolled printer box locators on the PrintersPage. These can be used during test setup to initiate enrollment or verify enrollment status. However, the core test scenario (all 15 test steps) requires components from the Print and Payment History page, which are not in this batch. API helpers for time manipulation, billing charge triggering, and page printing simulation are also not in this batch. The Print and Payment History page object, billing cycle card locators, progress bar locators, tooltip locators, and all verification methods for the main test flow are in different batches.
